title: regex match type usable without require ["regex"]
description: RFC 5228 §2.6 requires that extensions must be declared via require before use. The :regex match type is part of the "regex" extension, but check_extension_use in lib.rs only checks action commands (fileinto, reject) and the test word (envelope) in EXTENSION_TESTS. It never checks for Form::Tag("regex") in the test arguments. As a result, a script like: if header :regex "Subject" "test.*" { keep; } compiles and evaluates successfully without require ["regex"]. This silently violates RFC 5228 §2.6 and is confirmed by inspection of check_extension_use (lib.rs:220). The comment on line 217 says 'regex handled separately' but no separate handling exists. Fix: scan test argument tags for 'regex' in check_extension_use and require it to be declared, or handle it in a separate check pass over the form tree.
